herb_parts.name: root
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga root is known to have a diuretic effect, helping to increase urine production and relieve symptoms of edema and hypertension. The root also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing swelling and pain associated with various conditions. Furthermore, ammi visnaga root has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties.

herb_parts.name: leaf
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga leaf is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its expectorant properties, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and congestion. The leaf is also valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and reducing swelling. Additionally, ammi visnaga leaf has been used to treat digestive issues, including indigestion and bloating.

herb_parts.name: whole plant
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga whole plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including hypertension and bronchial asthma due to its active compound, furanocoumarin scopoletin. The plant is also employed for its expectorant properties, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Additionally, the whole plant of Ammi visnaga has been traditionally used for its diuretic properties to treat urinary retention.

herb_parts.name: rhizome
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga rhizome is known for its potential in treating various health conditions, particularly those related to the cardiovascular system. The rhizome has been traditionally used as a diuretic and to treat conditions such as hypertension and edema. Its medicinal properties are also thought to have an impact on reducing inflammation and promoting urine production.

herb_parts.name: aerial part
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga aerial part is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The aerial part of the plant is also used to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Additionally, it has been traditionally used to increase urine production and help with kidney issues.

herb_parts.name: seed
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga seed has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its expectorant and diuretic properties, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and congestion. The seed contains a bioactive compound called visnagin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. Additionally, the seed has been used to treat conditions like bronchitis and asthma, promoting the expulsion of mucus and reducing airway constriction.

herb_parts.name: stem
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga stem is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its coumarin content, which has been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and inflammation. The stem also contains other bioactive compounds, such as furanocoumarins, that have been studied for their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. These compounds may contribute to the stem's traditional use in herbal medicine for its antispasmodic and diuretic properties.

herb_parts.name: petiole
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga petiole, which is a part of the plant, contains a compound called khellin, a lignan glycoside that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The petiole of Ammi visnaga has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as asthma and angina. Additionally, the petiole has been used to treat other ailments, including fever and rheumatism, due to its ability to relax smooth muscles and improve blood flow.

herb_parts.name: flower
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga flower is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve flatulence. The flower is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the flower's diuretic properties make it useful in treating water retention and edema.

herb_parts.name: stem node
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga stem node has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and inflammation. The node is rich in coumarins, specifically umbelliferone and scopoletin, which possess anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. These compounds have been shown to have a positive effect on reducing fever and alleviating pain associated with inflammation.

herb_parts.name: inflorescence
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga inflorescence is used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory properties, as it contains sesquiterpenes and coumarins that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The inflorescence of Ammi visnaga has also been studied for its diuretic and vasodilatory effects, which can aid in lowering blood pressure and treating conditions such as hypertension. Additionally, it may possess antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating infections and wounds.

herb_parts.name: rhizome node
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga rhizome node contains coumarin, which is known for its anticoagulant and anti-inflammatory properties. It is also a rich source of sesquiterpenes, such as visnagin, which exhibit sedative and anti-anxiety effects. Additionally, the rhizome node of Ammi visnaga has been found to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties.

herb_parts.name: fruit
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga fruit is known to contain a compound called khellin, which has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The fruit also contains another compound called visnagin, which has been studied for its potential anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. These medicinal properties make the ammi visnaga fruit a valuable resource in natural medicine.

herb_parts.name: flower bud
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga flower bud has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. It is believed to contain sesquiterpenoid lactones, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. The bud's extracts are also thought to have antioxidant and antimicrobial activities.

herb_parts.name: tuber
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga tuber is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. It is also used to help reduce inflammation and as a diuretic to aid in the treatment of urinary issues like kidney stones and edema. The tuber's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of furanocoumarins, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

herb_parts.name: bark
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga bark, also known as Khella, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. The bark contains furanocoumarins and alkaloids, which may help alleviate respiratory issues such as asthma and bronchitis. Its medicinal properties are said to be beneficial in treating hypertension and other cardiovascular conditions.

herb_parts.name: seeds pod
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga seeds pod, which are used in traditional medicine, contain a number of bioactive compounds that have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The seeds of Ammi visnaga have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The pod of Ammi visnaga has been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery.

herb_parts.name: rootlet
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga rootlet, also known as Khella, is traditionally used in the treatment of hypertension and cardiac issues due to its vasodilatory properties. The rootlet is rich in furanocoumarins, particularly visnagin, which is responsible for its medicinal effects. It is often used in herbal medicine to alleviate symptoms associated with high blood pressure and heart disease.

herb_parts.name: corm
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga corm is a valuable medicinal part used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of cardiovascular and respiratory disorders. The corm is rich in ferulic acid and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties make ammi visnaga corm a potential natural remedy for various health conditions, including hypertension and bronchitis.

herb_parts.name: calyx
herb_parts.desc: Ammi visnaga calyx is known for its medicinal properties due to the presence of furanocoumarins, which contribute to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. The calyx of Ammi visnaga contains a unique compound called khellin, which is used in the treatment of bronchial asthma and other respiratory issues. Khellin also exhibits vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties, making the calyx of Ammi visnaga a valuable component in herbal medicine.
